meta.name: PHQ-8 Patient Health Questionnaire-8
meta.description: The PHQ-8 is an 8-item self-report measure of depressive symptoms, identical to the PHQ-9 but without the suicidality item. It is widely used in population-based studies and settings where assessment of self-harm is not appropriate or feasible.
meta.author: Kroenke, Strine, Spitzer, Williams, Berry & Mokdad (2009)
meta.reference: Kroenke K, Strine TW, Spitzer RL, Williams JBW, Berry JT, Mokdad AH. The PHQ-8 as a measure of current depression in the general population. J Affect Disord. 2009;114(1-3):163-173.
meta.instruction: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by any of the following problems?

questions.text: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
Feeling tired or having little energy
Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your depression score is within the normal range, indicating no clinically significant depressive symptoms over the past two weeks. Your current emotional well-being appears stable and healthy.
suggestions: Continue your current healthy habits including regular physical activity and social engagement
Maintain a balanced lifestyle with adequate sleep, nutrition, and time for activities you enjoy
Remember that occasional mood fluctuations are normal and typically resolve on their own

ranges.label: Mild Depression
ranges.description: You may be experiencing mild depressive symptoms that could subtly affect your energy, motivation, or enjoyment of daily activities. These symptoms are common and often improve with proactive self-care and lifestyle adjustments.
suggestions: Prioritize self-care by keeping a regular sleep schedule and incorporating daily physical activity into your routine
Reach out to a trusted friend or family member to share how you have been feeling
Practice stress reduction techniques such as mindfulness meditation, progressive muscle relaxation, or gentle yoga
If symptoms persist or worsen over the next few weeks, consider consulting a healthcare professional for guidance

ranges.label: Moderate Depression
ranges.description: Your depressive symptoms are at a moderate level and may be noticeably affecting your ability to function at work, maintain relationships, and manage daily responsibilities. Professional evaluation is advisable.
suggestions: Schedule an appointment with a mental health professional such as a therapist, counselor, or psychiatrist for evaluation
Establish and maintain a consistent daily routine to provide structure and a sense of accomplishment
Aim for regular physical exercise, which has well-documented benefits for improving depressive symptoms
Avoid relying on alcohol, caffeine, or other substances to manage your mood, as they often worsen symptoms
Consider evidence-based treatments such as cognitive behavioral therapy, which is highly effective for depression

ranges.label: Moderately Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your depressive symptoms are at a moderately severe level, likely causing significant impairment in your daily life, work, and personal relationships. Professional treatment is strongly recommended to support your recovery.
suggestions: Consult a psychiatrist or mental health professional promptly, as this level of symptoms typically requires professional intervention
A combined approach of medication and psychotherapy is often most effective for moderately severe depression
Ensure you have at least one trusted person in your support network who knows how you are feeling
Try to maintain social connections even when you feel like withdrawing, as isolation tends to worsen depression

ranges.label: Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your depressive symptoms are very severe and are likely causing major disruption to your daily functioning and overall quality of life. Immediate professional evaluation and treatment are strongly recommended.
suggestions: Please contact a mental health professional or visit a healthcare facility as soon as possible for urgent assessment
If you experience any thoughts of self-harm, reach out to a crisis helpline such as the 988 Lifeline immediately
Inform a trusted family member or friend about your situation so they can provide immediate support
Adhere closely to any treatment plans recommended by your healthcare providers, including prescribed medications
Understand that severe depression is a medical condition that responds well to treatment, and improvement is achievable
